title: Resident Doctors in England End Long-Running Strikes
subtitle: A new agreement on pay and training marks the end of three years of industrial action in the NHS.

summary.en: Resident doctors in England have voted to accept a government offer, ending three years of strikes. The deal includes pay raises and 4,500 new training positions. Patients have faced many cancelled appointments during the long dispute. Both the government and the British Medical Association expressed relief at the resolution. The focus now shifts to implementing the changes and stabilizing the health service.
summary.zh: 英格蘭的住院醫師已投票接受政府提出的方案，結束了長達三年的罷工。該協議包含加薪與四千五百個新的培訓職位。在這場漫長的爭議中，病患面臨許多預約被取消的情況。政府與英國醫學會皆對此決議感到欣慰。目前的焦點已轉向落實變革並穩定醫療服務。

paragraphs.en: Resident doctors in England have officially voted to accept a new government deal regarding pay and working conditions. This decision brings an end to three years of industrial action—a period where workers stop performing their duties to protest—that has significantly impacted the National Health Service (NHS). The agreement follows a referendum of British Medical Association (BMA) members, where 53% of those who voted supported the proposal.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭的住院醫師已正式投票通過政府提出的薪資與工作條件新協議。這項決定終結了長達三年的罷工行動——即勞工停止執行職務以示抗議的時期——該行動對英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）造成了重大影響。此協議是在英國醫學會（BMA）成員進行投票後達成的，其中百分之五十三的投票者支持該提案。

paragraphs.en: According to the BBC and The Guardian, the turnout for the vote was 57%, with over 32,000 doctors participating. This resolution concludes a difficult chapter for the NHS, which has seen hundreds of thousands of patient appointments and operations cancelled since the first strikes began in March 2023. The cost of this disruption has been high, with The Guardian reporting that the strikes cost the health service approximately £1 billion since last summer.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司（BBC）與衛報（The Guardian）報導，此次投票率為百分之五十七，超過三萬兩千名醫師參與。這項決議為國家醫療保健服務艱難的一章畫下句點，自二零二三年三月首次罷工以來，該體系已有數十萬次病患預約與手術被迫取消。衛報指出，這場混亂代價高昂，自去年夏天以來，罷工已導致醫療服務損失約十億英鎊。

paragraphs.en: Resident doctors are qualified medical professionals who have completed their initial degree and are now gaining further experience or specializing in areas like surgery or emergency medicine. They make up nearly half of all doctors in England. Previously known as junior doctors, the government officially changed their title to resident doctors in September 2024 to better recognize their level of expertise.
paragraphs.zh: 住院醫師是指已完成初步學位，目前正在累積進一步經驗或專攻外科、急診醫學等領域的合格醫療專業人員。他們佔英格蘭所有醫師人數的近半數。這些醫師過去被稱為初級醫師，政府於二零二四年九月正式將其職稱改為住院醫師，以更貼切地肯定他們的專業水準。

paragraphs.en: The new deal offers a variety of improvements. Beyond a pay increase, which will see average salaries rise, the package includes 4,500 extra training places for newly qualified doctors over the next three years. The government has also agreed to cover out-of-pocket expenses for medical exams. Under the terms of the deal, resident doctors will receive backdated pay to April 2026. The BMA noted that by April 2027, the average pay increase will reach 6.6%, with further adjustments planned for the future.
paragraphs.zh: 這項新協議提供了多項改善措施。除了調升平均薪資外，該方案還包含在未來三年內為新進合格醫師增加四千五百個培訓名額。政府也同意負擔醫師的醫療考試自付費用。根據協議條款，住院醫師將獲得回溯至二零二六年四月的薪資補發。英國醫學會指出，到二零二七年四月，平均薪資漲幅將達到百分之六點六，未來並有進一步調整的計畫。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Jack Fletcher, the chair of the BMA’s resident doctors committee, stated that the strikes were unnecessary and that a solution could have been reached much sooner. He emphasized that the deal provides a better-staffed NHS and improved conditions for doctors. However, he also noted that while this agreement is a significant step, it is not the end of the road for their goals regarding long-term pay restoration.
paragraphs.zh: 英國醫學會住院醫師委員會主席傑克·弗萊徹（Jack Fletcher）醫師表示，罷工本無必要，若能更早達成解決方案會更好。他強調，這項協議能為國家醫療保健服務提供更充足的人力，並改善醫師的工作條件。然而他也指出，雖然這項協議是重要的一步，但對於他們長期薪資恢復的目標而言，這並非終點。

paragraphs.en: Health and Social Care Secretary James Murray welcomed the news, saying it allows the government to stop the disruption and focus on rebuilding the health service. He acknowledged that there is still much work to be done to improve the working lives of NHS staff. Dean Royles, the interim chief executive of NHS Employers, expressed relief that a resolution had been found, noting that the hard work of putting these new policies into practice will now begin.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生與社會照護大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞（James Murray）對此消息表示歡迎，稱這讓政府能停止混亂，並專注於重建醫療服務。他承認，要改善國家醫療保健服務員工的工作生活，仍有許多工作要做。國家醫療保健服務雇主組織（NHS Employers）臨時執行長狄恩·羅伊爾斯（Dean Royles）則對達成決議感到欣慰，並指出將新政策付諸實行的艱鉅工作現在才要開始。

paragraphs.en: The situation for doctors varies across the United Kingdom. In Scotland, resident doctors have already accepted a pay offer. Meanwhile, in Wales, doctors are currently discussing their own pay and training concerns without having gone on strike. In Northern Ireland, however, the situation remains tense, with resident doctors planning a 24-hour strike.
paragraphs.zh: 醫師面臨的情況在英國各地有所不同。在蘇格蘭，住院醫師已經接受了薪資方案。同時，在威爾斯，醫師們目前正在討論各自的薪資與培訓問題，且尚未採取罷工行動。然而，在北愛爾蘭，情況依然緊張，住院醫師正計畫進行二十四小時的罷工。

paragraphs.en: For patients in England, the end of the strikes offers hope for a more stable period. The NHS has faced immense pressure, and the cancellation of appointments has been a major concern for the public. With the dispute now settled, the focus moves toward implementing the ambitious timeline of the new contract. Both the government and the BMA have expressed a desire to work together to ensure the health service remains fit for the future, though they acknowledge that the path ahead will require continued effort from all sides.
paragraphs.zh: 對於英格蘭的病患而言，罷工的結束帶來了邁向更穩定時期的希望。國家醫療保健服務面臨巨大壓力，預約取消一直是公眾關注的主要問題。隨著爭議平息，焦點轉向落實新合約的宏大時程表。政府與英國醫學會皆表達了共同合作的意願，以確保醫療服務能適應未來需求，儘管雙方都承認，前方的道路仍需要各方持續努力。

keyTerms.definitionEn: The point at which something must stop or finish.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 盡頭；終點；無法再繼續下去的時刻。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的隱喻用法，並非指真正的道路盡頭。文中：However, he also noted that while this agreement is a significant step, it is not the end of the road for their goals regarding long-term pay restoration.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Suitable or appropriate for a particular purpose.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 合適的；勝任的；準備好的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（適合）或名詞（健康），這裡作形容詞用，表示「足以應付」。文中：Both the government and the BMA have expressed a desire to work together to ensure the health service remains fit for the future, though they acknowledge that the path ahead will require continued effort from all sides.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To provide enough money to pay for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（遮蓋），這裡在財務語境下指「負擔費用」。文中：The government has also agreed to cover out-of-pocket expenses for medical exams.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: Resident doctors in England accept pay deal and end strikes

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Resident doctors in England accept government offer on pay and jobs

quiz.question: According to the article, what is one specific benefit included in the new deal for resident doctors besides a salary increase?

options.A: A guaranteed reduction in the number of weekly working hours.
options.B: The provision of 4,500 additional training opportunities.
options.C: Immediate pay raises effective from March 2023.
options.D: A formal apology from the Health and Social Care Secretary.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，新協議不僅包含加薪，還承諾在未來三年內為新合格的醫生提供 4,500 個額外的培訓名額。選項 A 並未在文中提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為文中提到加薪回溯至 2026 年 4 月，而非 2023 年；選項 D 雖然提到秘書長對此表示歡迎，但文中並未提及有任何「正式道歉」。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future of the NHS based on the statements from Dr. Jack Fletcher and James Murray?

options.A: The NHS will immediately return to full operational capacity without any further issues.
options.B: The government plans to privatize the NHS to avoid future industrial action.
options.C: Both parties recognize that the current agreement is only a starting point for further improvements.
options.D: The BMA intends to resume strikes if the 6.6% pay increase is not met by April 2026.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段與第六段，Dr. Jack Fletcher 提到這不是爭取目標的終點，而 James Murray 也承認仍有許多工作要做以改善員工的工作生活。這顯示雙方都認為目前協議僅是改善過程中的一步，而非最終解決方案。選項 A 過於樂觀，文中提到仍需持續努力；選項 B 文中完全未提及；選項 D 錯誤，因為 6.6% 的目標日期是 2027 年，且文中未提及會立即恢復罷工。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, the phrase 'fit for the future' is used to describe the desired state of the NHS. What does this mean in context?

options.A: Equipped and prepared to handle upcoming challenges effectively.
options.B: Physically healthy enough to participate in sports activities.
options.C: Reduced in size to match the current government budget.
options.D: Strictly following the traditional methods of the past.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，「fit for the future」意指該機構具備足夠的能力、資源或結構，能夠適應並應對未來的挑戰與需求。選項 B 是對「fit」一詞的字面誤解；選項 C 與文中提到的「重建」與「增加培訓」目標相悖；選項 D 錯誤，因為協議的目的是為了改進現狀，而非固守舊法。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main point of the article?

options.A: The BMA has successfully forced the government to privatize medical training.
options.B: Resident doctors in England have ended a long period of strikes by accepting a new government pay and conditions deal.
options.C: The NHS is currently in a state of total collapse due to ongoing strikes in Northern Ireland.
options.D: Resident doctors across the entire United Kingdom have reached a unified agreement with the government.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。整篇文章的核心在於報導英格蘭住院醫師投票接受政府協議，從而結束了長達三年的工業行動（罷工）。選項 A 錯誤，協議內容與私有化無關；選項 C 過度誇大，文章僅提到北愛爾蘭情況緊張，並未說 NHS 處於崩潰狀態；選項 D 錯誤，文章第七段明確指出各地區（如蘇格蘭、威爾斯、北愛爾蘭）的情況各異，並非統一協議。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
